normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 1.5 - Energy Efficiency in Building Design
scope_summary: Local planning policy encouraging environmentally sustainable and energy efficient design principles in all development within the City of Melville. Covers building orientation/passive solar design, solar access protection (including strata lot development), waterwise landscaping and tree retention, and building infrastructure initiatives such as rainwater tanks, greywater systems, solar water heaters, photovoltaics, insulation and roof colours. Provisions are framed as encouragement ('encouraged', 'are to have regard to') rather than mandatory numeric standards; the City applies them case by case alongside the R-Codes.

cross_references.cited: State Planning Policy 7.3 Residential Design Codes Volume 1
Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
Water Corporation of Western Australia (waterwise plant definitions)

quality_flags: clean extraction, 4 pages, full text legible
policy is advisory/encouragement-based: no mandatory measurable development standards to capture
document footer shows 'This Version: 31/10/2019' print date; operative version is the 6 August 2019 administrative review (Council Resolution 18/6/2019)
